person.name: Dreux IV de MELLO

memoryArtifacts.headline: mello D'or, à deux fasces de gueules, accompagnée de neuf merlettes du même, rangées en orle.

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
Armoiries de la famille de Mello :
D'or, à deux fasces de gueules, accompagnée de neuf merlettes du même, rangées en orle.
Dreux de Mello, seigneur de Sainte-Hermine ;
Décédé en 1311.
Il est fils de Dreux de Mello, seigneur d'Époisses et de Eustachie de Lusignan.
Marié en premières noces  en 1305, avec Eleonora di Savoia (1280 - 1324), ils eurent :
 1) Marguerite ;
Marié en secondes noces avec Jeanne de Toucy ; ils eurent :
 2) Jeanne ( - 1351)

relevé JPR Teyssier Chalancon Mangas

zh.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO是Dreux III de MELLO的兒子，於大約1255年出生在法國豪-德-福朗斯奧易梅洛。他於1292年2月10日與Eléonore de SAVOIE在法國i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲結婚。他們至少是2個女兒的父母。他去世於1318年4月3日在法國布格-福朗-可特艾尼艾福a奧-農享年64歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO是Dreux III de MELLO的儿子，于大约1255年出生在法国豪-德-福朗斯奧易梅洛。他于1292年2月10日与Eléonore de SAVOIE在法国i萊-德-福朗斯芭黎絲结婚。他们至少是2个女儿的父母。他去世于1318年4月3日在法国布格-福朗-可特艾尼艾福a奧-農享年64岁。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.
Indian: variant of   Dey  .

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: MELLO
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Manuel, Jose, Carlos, Adelino, Ana, Joaquin, Acacio, Claudio, Cristina, Fernando, Americo, Enrique.  Portuguese Duarte, Joaquim, Armanda, Joao, Paulo, Serafim.
Italian: probably from a short form of a personal name ending with the hypocoristic suffix -ello, such as Giacomello from Giacomo .
Portuguese: variant of   Melo  .

variants.name: Giacomo
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Domenico, Gino, Salvatore.
Italian: from the personal name Giacomo, from Latin Jacobus via Late Latin Jacomus (see James and Jacob ).

variants.name: Melle
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  German Heinz, Horst, Kurt, Otto.
Italian: habitational name from Melle in Cuneo province (Piedmont); in some cases possibly also a variant of a personal name such as   Mello   1.
Norwegian: probably a variant of   Mele  .

variants.name: Mellone
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Italian Antonio, Emidio, Luigi, Pasquale.
Italian: from an augmentative of   Mello  .

nameMeanings.name: Dreux
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland © University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Channel Islands:  See  Druce  .

variants.name: Druce
meanings: Transferred use of the Scottish surname, now used as a given name throughout the English-speaking world. In the 20th century it was particularly popular in Australia. The surname was originally a Norman baronial name, but a precise identification of the place from which it was derived has not been made (there are a large number of possible candidates). The Bruces were an influential Norman family in Scottish affairs in the early Middle Ages; its most famous member was  Robert  ‘the Bruce’ (  1274–1329 ), who is said to have drawn inspiration after his defeat at Methven from the perseverance of a spider in repeatedly climbing up again after being knocked down. He ruled Scotland as  King Robert I  from  1306 to 1329 .

nameMeanings.name: IV
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Short form of   Violet  ,   Vivien  , or   Vivian .

variants.name: Vivien
meanings: Earlier generally taken as a variant of the boy's name Vivian , but now also rarely used for boys. This spelling was quite common in Old French. Its use as a girl's name in the English-speaking world was influenced by Tennyson's Merlin and Vivien ( 1859 ). This name, from Arthurian legend, may represent an altered form of a Celtic name (perhaps akin to the Irish Gaelic name Béibhinn ‘white lady’, pronounced ‘bee-veen’). The actress Vivien Leigh ( 1913–67 ) was christened Vivian.

variants.name: Violet
meanings: From the name of the flower (Old French violette, Late Latin violetta, a diminutive of viola). This was one of the earliest flower names to become popular in Britain, being used as early as 1700 and becoming well established in the 19th century, although it is now somewhat out of favour.

variants.name: Vivian
meanings: Originally a boy's name, from an Old French form of the Latin name Vivianus (probably a derivative of vivus ‘alive’), but now more frequent as a girl's name. The name was borne by a 5th-century bishop of Saintes in western France, remembered for protecting his people during the invasion of the Visigoths.

en.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO was born about 1255, in Mello, Oise, Hauts-de-France, France as the son of Dreux III de MELLO. He married Eléonore de SAVOIE on 10 February 1292, in Paris, Île-de-France, France. They were the parents of at least 2 daughters. He died on 3 April 1318, in Château-Chinon, Nièvre, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, at the age of 64.

fr.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO est né environ 1255, à Mello, Oise, Hauts-de-France, France, fils de Dreux III de MELLO. Il a épousé Eléonore de SAVOIE le 10 février 1292, à Paris, Île-de-France, France. Ils ont eu au moins 2 filles. Il est décédé le 3 avril 1318, à Château-Chinon, Nièvre, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, France, à l’âge de 64 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO wurde um 1255 in Mello, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Frankreich, als Sohn von Dreux III de MELLO geboren. Er hat am 10. Februar 1292 in Paris, Île-de-France, Frankreich Eléonore de SAVOIE geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 2 Töchter. Er starb am 3. April 1318 im Alter von 64 Jahren in Château-Chinon, Nièvre, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Frankreich.

it.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO è nato il circa 1255 nel Mello, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Francia come figlio di Dreux III de MELLO. Lui sposato Eléonore de SAVOIE il 10 febbraio 1292, nel Paris, Île-de-France, Francia. Erano genitori di almeno 2 figlie. Morì il 3 aprile 1318, nel Château-Chinon, Nièvre, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, all'età di 64 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLOは約1255年にフランスハウトス-デ-フランスオイゼメロでDreux III de MELLOの息子として生まれた。1292年2月10日にフランスiレ-デ-フランスパーイスでEléonore de SAVOIEと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも2人の娘の親であった。彼は1318年4月3日にフランスボウルゴグネ-フランチェ-コムトeニevアチaテアウ-チノンで64歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO(은)는 대략 1255년 프랑스 하츠-데-프랜스 이스 멜로에서 Dreux III de MELLO의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 1292년 2월 10일 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 파리스에서 Eléonore de SAVOIE(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1318년 4월 3일 프랑스 부르고그니-프랭체-코트e 니e브르 cha테오-친넌에서 64세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO nasceu aproximadamente 1255, em Mello, Oise, Hauts-de-France, França filho de Dreux III de MELLO. Ele casou-se com Eléonore de SAVOIE em 10 de fevereiro de 1292, em Paris, Île-de-France, França. Eles tiveram pelo menos 2 filhas. Ele faleceu em 3 de abril de 1318, em Château-Chinon, Nièvre, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, França, com 64 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO родился около 1255 г. в местности меллоу, оис, хауц-де-фрэнс, Франция. Его отца звали Dreux III de MELLO. Он женился на женщине по имени Eléonore de SAVOIE 10 февраля 1292 г. в местности пэрис, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция. У них было по крайней мере 2 дочери. Он умер 3 апреля 1318 г. в местности чaто-чинон, ниeвр, бургогне-фрэнче-комтe, Франция в возрасте 64 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Dreux IV de MELLO nació aproximadamente 1255, en Mello, Oise, Hauts-de-France, Francia como hijo de Dreux III de MELLO. Se casó con Eléonore de SAVOIE el 10 de febrero de 1292, en Paris, Île-de-France, Francia. Fueron padres de por lo menos 2 hijas. Murió el 3 de abril de 1318, en Château-Chinon, Nièvre, Bourgogne-Franche-Comté, Francia, a la edad de 64 años.
